InVivo_Result_Summary: Significantly greater tumor accumulation and increased survival in orthotopic GBM mice

Notes: Notes
Title: Title
Curator Note: Curator Note

N_Terminal_Modification: Replacement of Tyr1 by Dmt (2',6'-dimethyl-(S)-tyrosine) at the N-terminus
Chemical_Modifications: Dmt at position 1; N-methylated D-Ala at position 2 (NMe-D-Ala2); Aba (4-amino-1,2,4,5-tetrahydro-2-benzazepin-3-one) at position 3
Notes: Opioid tetrapeptide BVD03 (H-Dmt-NMe-D-Ala-Aba-Gly-NH2) is a dermorphin-derived ligand built on a H-Dmt1-Xxx2-Yyy3-Gly4-NH2 scaffold with Dmt1 to improve enzymatic stability, receptor affinity and potency, N-methylated D-Ala2 to increase lipophilicity, and a conformationally constrained Aba benzazepine side chain in position 3 that maintains MOR affinity and increases DOR binding and activity. Tetrapeptide analogues of this type are reported to be transported into the brain after intravenous and subcutaneous administration and to activate μ- and δ-opioid receptors more efficiently and for longer periods of time than morphine. In vivo BVD03 shows dose- and time-dependent antinociceptive activity in the hot water tail-flick test in male C57Bl6 mice after i.v. administration at doses from 0.25 to 4 mg/kg; at 4 mg/kg it produces weaker analgesia than morphine at the same dose, with %MPE 68.78 ± 7.18 at 60 min and 50.06 ± 6.84 at 120 min, while at 1 and 0.5 mg/kg it produces high %MPE values (89.21 ± 5.22 and 86.69 ± 5.52 at 60 and 120 min for 1 mg/kg; 88.41 ± 7.15 and 84.29 ± 6.46 at 60 and 120 min for 0.5 mg/kg). At 0.25 mg/kg BVD03 shows %MPE 35–40% at 15 min, with a gradual increase to a maximum %MPE of 57.48 ± 17.57 at 120 min. Repeated daily i.v. administration of 4 mg/kg for 5 days induces tolerance, with low MPE values by day 5. Overall BVD03 is less potent than AN81 but retains central antinociceptive activity after systemic administration, indicating BBB penetration.
Model_Type: Mouse acute thermal nociception model (hot water tail-flick assay)
Disease_or_Physical_Condition: Acute pain model (thermal nociception in otherwise healthy mice)
Papp: Hot water tail-flick test; % maximal possible effect (%MPE)
Outcome_Type: Tetrapeptide analogues of the type H-Dmt1-Xxx2-Yyy3-Gly4-NH2, including BVD03, are reported to be transported into the brain after intravenous and subcutaneous administration; systemic BVD03 produces centrally mediated antinociception consistent with BBB penetration.
Outcome_Value: Antinociception (%MPE in hot water tail-flick test)
Outcome_Unit: For BVD03 at 0.25 mg/kg i.v., %MPE is 35–40% at 15 min with a gradual increase to a maximum %MPE of 57.48 ± 17.57 at 120 min.
Transport_Type: Produces dose- and time-dependent analgesic (antinociceptive) effects in an acute thermal pain model; weaker than morphine at equimolar dose but with clear central opioid-mediated analgesia.
Subcellular_Localisation: μ-opioid receptor agonist; δ-opioid receptor agonist
Chemical_Label: Peptide dissolved in physiological saline (0.9% NaCl) for intravenous injection
InVitro_Result_Summary: Dermorphin-derived tetrapeptide with Dmt1 and Aba3 conferring high MOR affinity and increased DOR binding and activity; BVD03 is less potent in vivo than AN81 but still produces robust antinociception after systemic administration.
InVivo_Result_Summary: Previous in vitro studies (summarized in Table 1) identified BVD03 (H-Dmt-NMe-D-Ala-Aba-Gly-NH2) as a potent opioid tetrapeptide derivative: replacement of Tyr1 by Dmt and introduction of the Aba benzazepine scaffold in position 3 maintained high μ-opioid receptor affinity and activation and led to a favorable increase in δ-opioid receptor binding and activity compared with dermorphin-derived analogues.
Label_Confidence: In male C57Bl6 mice, intravenous BVD03 at 0.25–4 mg/kg produced time- and dose-dependent antinociception in the 55°C hot water tail-flick test with measurements at 5, 15, 30, 60 and 120 min. At 4 mg/kg BVD03 showed weaker analgesia than morphine (4 mg/kg), with a peak %MPE of 68.78 ± 7.18 at 60 min and a decline to 50.06 ± 6.84 at 120 min; at 1 and 0.5 mg/kg it produced high %MPE values (about 84–89% at 60–120 min). At 0.25 mg/kg, antinociception started at 35–40% MPE at 15 min and increased to a maximum of 57.48 ± 17.57% MPE at 120 min. Chronic i.v. administration of 4 mg/kg once daily for 5 days induced marked tolerance, with low %MPE by day 5. The study concludes that these tetrapeptide analogues, including BVD03, cross the BBB after systemic administration and activate central opioid receptors to produce analgesia.
Year: In vivo antinociception of potent mu opioid agonist tetrapeptide analogues and comparison with a compact opioid agonist-neurokinin 1 receptor antagonist chimera
Curator Note: https://molecularbrain.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/1756-6606-5-4

Notes: Macrocyclic peptide designed to test the effect of cyclization on $\text{BBB}$ permeability.
